diagrams.title: Hash Table with Chaining
diagrams.description: Shows a hash table where multiple elements with same hash address are stored as linked lists in each bucket
elements: Each bucket points to linked list head
Elements 15, 27, 43 all hash to index 3 forming a chain
Visual representation of separate chaining collision resolution
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how chaining handles collisions by storing multiple elements per bucket using linked lists

diagrams.title: Linear Probing Collision Resolution
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how linear probing resolves collisions by searching consecutive positions
elements: Probe sequence shown as arrows to next available slot
Primary clustering visualization with long filled sequences
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how linear probing handles collisions and understanding the clustering problem

diagrams.title: Hash Function Mapping Process
diagrams.description: Shows the complete process of converting a key to hash table address
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the mapping from key to storage location and how hash functions compute addresses

diagrams.title: Open Addressing vs Chaining Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison of both collision resolution techniques
elements: Chaining: bucket array with linked lists attached
Open Addressing: single-level array with probing arrows
Load factor impact on both techniques
diagrams.purpose: Comparing the two main approaches to collision resolution and their structural differences
